minimal.summary: GitOps Review for repo hygiene, commit quality, PR readiness, CI state, packaging, changelog, merge, tag, push, and release gates across GitHub and GitLab.
required_inputs: target gate: commit, PR, merge, release, or cleanup
remote context when GitHub or GitLab parity matters
explicit execution permission when mutating git or release state
expected_outputs: exact branch, tag, PR, or MR identifiers

notes: wrapper surfaces are additive deployment forms, not capability types
orchestrators must not infer execution authority from wrapper presence alone

install_target.rationale: Source lives inside the current repository, so repo-local installation is the safer default.

minimal.rationale: Source carries both strong workflow/prompt structure and explicit agent-only control semantics. Keep one SSOT entry and emit both workflow and agent surfaces.
minimal.display_name: GitOps Review — Repo Hygiene, CI, Release, and Merge Gate

relationship_suggestions: prefer explicit review or analyst companions before high-authority use
pair with review and analysis capabilities when converging design proposals
migration_notes: If the capability lands, compare it against existing entries before apply.
Relationship suggestions are advisory only and do not imply runtime routing.
adjustment_recommendations: add an explicit primary objective before packaging
add deterministic in-scope sections to improve routing fitness

org_graph.work_graph_impact: Adds or updates a both capability with route profile SSOT_AUDIT.

display_name: GitOps Review — Repo Hygiene, CI, Release, and Merge Gate
invocation_hints: judge the repo organization or GitOps hygiene
make sure my commits are logically scoped and messages are strong
check whether my PR is ready to merge
confirm CI is green on GitHub and GitLab
merge, push, package, tag, release, or clean up branches
update changelog or release notes as part of a release gate
shared_summary: GitOps Review for repo hygiene, commit quality, PR readiness, CI state, packaging, changelog, merge, tag, push, and release gates across GitHub and GitLab.
shared_constraints: Do not hide destructive git behavior behind helper language.
Do not claim CI is green unless the current hosted runs were verified.
Do not merge, tag, package, or release without stating the prerequisite checks.
Do not invent GitHub or GitLab state from local git alone.
Do not replace companion capability reviews with generic “looks good” language.

benchmark_sources.label: Code Review benchmark
benchmark_sources.note: Benchmark for commit quality and review rigor.

benchmark_sources.label: UAC Import benchmark
benchmark_sources.note: Benchmark for deterministic workflow contracts and machine-readable metadata.

preferred_use_cases: merge, tag, package, and release flow checks

historical_baseline.reason: Only version currently present in repo history; use as default oracle until a stronger historical body exists.
